skill.name: zach-seller-skill-creator
skill.description: Create, validate, and benchmark Claude/Codex skills with a Chinese-first workflow tailored for Amazon sellers. Enforces a 6-question business gate before generating any skill definition.
skill.summary: Chinese-language skill creator for Amazon sellers with built-in business validation, testing, and HTML reporting tools.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 757 patterns (risk score 100/100), but the vast majority are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' hits are Chinese markdown backticks for code formatting. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' hits are documentation mentions of the word 'hash' or file extension lists. Real risk factors include subprocess.Popen calls in scripts/run_eval.py (for running `claude -p` during evaluation), innerHTML assignments in viewer.html (XSS potential if rendering untrusted eval data), and file archive operations in scripts/package_skill.py. No malicious intent found; all patterns serve the skill's stated purpose of creating, testing, and packaging other skills.

high_findings.title: innerHTML assignment in eval viewer
high_findings.description: assets/eval_review.html and eval-viewer/viewer.html contain innerHTML assignments that could allow XSS if rendering untrusted evaluation data. The data is locally generated from the skill's own eval pipeline, so practical risk is low, but the pattern is present.

high_findings.title: subprocess.Popen for claude CLI evaluation
high_findings.description: scripts/run_eval.py uses subprocess.Popen to spawn the `claude` CLI for running skill evaluations. This is a legitimate use case for an evaluation tool, but it does execute external commands based on skill query input.

medium_findings.title: Python archive operations for skill packaging
medium_findings.description: scripts/package_skill.py uses zipfile/shutil to package skills. This is standard functionality for a skill creation tool but could be misused to archive sensitive files if used outside its intended scope.

medium_findings.title: File write operations for benchmark reports
medium_findings.description: scripts/aggregate_benchmark.py writes aggregated benchmark data to files. Standard reporting functionality for the evaluation pipeline.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access in eval runner
medium_findings.description: scripts/run_eval.py reads os.environ to manage CLAUDECODE variable for nested claude invocations. Legitimate but environment-aware.

low_findings.title: Fetch API calls in evaluation viewer
low_findings.description: eval-viewer/viewer.html uses fetch() to load local JSON data files. Local file access only, not external network requests.

low_findings.title: Temporary file and directory usage
low_findings.description: scripts/run_loop.py creates temporary files for evaluation loops. Standard practice for sandboxed test execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown backtick false positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The vast majority of 'external_commands' findings are Chinese markdown documentation using backticks for code formatting (e.g., `skill-creator`, `SKILL.md`, `claude -p`). These are not code execution patterns. Confidence: 0.95 false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Cryptographic keyword false positives
dangerous_patterns.description: The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are triggered by documentation text mentioning file hashes, checksums, or the word 'crypto' in context unrelated to actual cryptographic operations. These are not real crypto implementations. Confidence: 0.92 false positive.

content.user_title: Create Amazon Seller Skills in Chinese
content.value_statement: Amazon sellers struggle to turn business knowledge into reusable AI skills. This skill enforces a 6-question gate before writing any skill definition, ensuring output has real business depth.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through a 6-question business gate (goal, past approach, steps, methodology, invocation, expected output) before creating any skill
Generates SKILL.md files following the Anthropic skill specification with Chinese-first documentation
Runs parallel evaluations comparing skill-enabled output against baseline output
Produces HTML visualization reports of benchmark results for easy comparison
Packages validated skills into distributable zip archives
Provides template references in Chinese for common Amazon seller workflows (keyword analysis, ad optimization, listing review)
limitations: Requires users to answer 6 business questions before proceeding; cannot skip the gate even with explicit override requests
Evaluation pipeline requires the `claude` CLI to be installed and accessible in PATH
InnerHTML-based report viewer may not sanitize all edge cases in generated evaluation data
Chinese-first design may not be optimal for non-Chinese-speaking Amazon sellers

use_cases.title: Amazon operations team automates keyword research
use_cases.description: An Amazon seller wants to turn their manual keyword analysis process into a reusable skill. This creator asks the 6 business questions, then builds and tests the skill.

use_cases.title: Solopreneur packages their ad optimization workflow
use_cases.description: A solo Amazon seller documents their PPC campaign optimization steps. The skill generates a validated, packaged automation they can share with their team.

use_cases.title: Agency builds a library of client-facing skills
use_cases.description: An e-commerce agency creates multiple skills for different clients (listing review, inventory alerts, competitor monitoring) using a consistent, tested workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a new skill from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create a new skill for [describe your Amazon workflow]. Please ask me the 6 business questions first, then build the skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user starting a new skill project

prompt_templates.title: Improve an existing skill
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an existing skill at [path/to/SKILL.md] that isn't performing well. Help me improve it using the analysis and improvement workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a skill that needs optimization

prompt_templates.title: Run benchmark evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Run an evaluation comparing my skill [skill-name] against the baseline on these test cases: [list test scenarios]. Generate an HTML report.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to measure skill effectiveness

prompt_templates.title: Package and distribute a skill
prompt_templates.prompt: My skill is ready for distribution. Package it into a zip file with proper validation and generate a description optimized for the skill marketplace.
prompt_templates.scenario: User ready to publish or share their skill

output_examples.input: 我想做一个帮我分析竞品 listing 的 skill
output: 1. 业务目标：通过分析竞品 listing 提升自己的转化率
2. 过去做法：手动复制粘贴到 Excel 对比
3. 具体步骤：提取标题/五点/A+/价格 → 分类对比 → 输出差异报告
4. 方法论：关注转化率相关的元素（图片质量、卖点表达）
5. 调用方式：粘贴 ASIN 或 listing 链接
6. 期望输出：对比表格 + 3 条优化建议
SKILL.md 已生成，包含完整工作流定义和测试用例。

output_examples.input: Run benchmark for my keyword-research skill

best_practices: Always answer the 6 business questions completely before requesting skill generation; partial answers lead to low-quality output
Run evaluations with at least 2-3 test cases to get meaningful comparison data between skill-enabled and baseline performance
Use the generated HTML reports to identify specific failure patterns and iterate on the skill definition
anti_patterns: Do not skip the 6-question gate even if you think your request is clear; the gate prevents vague skill definitions
Do not run evaluations without first having a complete SKILL.md file; partial definitions produce misleading benchmark results
Do not modify the viewer.html innerHTML rendering logic unless you understand the XSS implications for untrusted eval data

faq.question: What makes this different from the official Anthropic skill-creator?
faq.answer: This version adds a 6-question business gate before skill creation, forcing users to define business context, past approaches, and expected outputs. It prevents vague skill definitions that are common when Amazon sellers (with deep business knowledge but limited AI experience) try to document workflows.

faq.question: Do I need to speak Chinese to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill is designed Chinese-first for Amazon sellers who prefer Chinese documentation. However, it can process English inputs and generate English skill definitions if the user prefers.

faq.question: What does the evaluation pipeline do?
faq.answer: It runs your skill against a set of test cases, then runs a baseline (without skill) against the same cases. It generates an HTML report showing pass/fail rates, response times, and specific failure patterns so you can iterate on the skill definition.

faq.question: Can I skip the 6-question gate?
faq.answer: No. The gate is enforced as a hard requirement. Even if you explicitly request to skip it, the skill will not proceed to SKILL.md generation until all 6 questions are answered with substantive responses.

faq.question: What tools are required to run evaluations?
faq.answer: You need the Claude CLI installed and accessible in your PATH. The evaluation script uses subprocess to spawn `claude -p` for running test cases.

faq.question: How do I package and distribute my completed skill?
faq.answer: Use the `scripts/package_skill.py` utility after your skill passes evaluation. It creates a zip archive with proper validation checks, and `scripts/improve_description.py` can optimize the skill description for marketplace discoverability.
